title: Dead export: notifyStatediffSubscribers has zero callers anywhere

findingDetails.evidence: `rg -nw notifyStatediffSubscribers` over the whole repo returns exactly one hit — the export at statediff.ts:230. No production caller and not even a test references it, unlike sibling exports planStatediff/applyStatediffActions which at least have statediff.vitest.ts coverage.
findingDetails.why: A public exported async function with no caller and no test is unreachable code that adds maintenance surface and falsely implies a subscriber-notification path exists in the statediff reconciliation layer.
findingDetails.recommendation: Delete notifyStatediffSubscribers, or wire it into applyStatediffActions if subscriber notification is actually intended; if kept as staged foundation, add a test and a comment noting it is not yet wired.

title: Dead module: resolveEdgeTierBasement (edge-tier-basement.ts) only consumed by a test

findingDetails.evidence: resolveEdgeTierBasement and the re-export EDGE_TIER_STATE_LIMITS are the module's only exports; the sole importer is feedback-retention-reducer.vitest.ts:8. The wired reducer feedback-retention-reducer.ts and memory-retention-sweep.ts (which DO import evaluateFeedbackRetention/recordFeedbackRetentionAudit) never import edge-tier-basement.
findingDetails.why: The edge-floor retention decision the impl-summary (004-retention-reducer/implementation-summary.md:42,65) presents as built never runs against any production retention candidate — the helper is exercised only by unit tests, so the described causal-edge durability protection is not actually applied.
findingDetails.recommendation: Wire resolveEdgeTierBasement into the retention sweep's edge path, or remove the module and the corresponding impl-summary claim if edge flooring is deferred.

title: Dead export: codeHash() helper has no production call site

findingDetails.evidence: `rg -n 'codeHash\('` shows the function invoked only in tests (canonical-fingerprint.vitest.ts:21-22, incremental-index-foundation.vitest.ts:30,76,91). Production incremental-index.ts imports canonicalFingerprint but not codeHash, and supplies input.codeHash from elsewhere (incremental-index.ts:745 reads input.codeHash, never calls the helper).
findingDetails.why: An exported hashing helper intended for memo/code-version invalidation that no production path uses is dead code; it can drift from how production actually computes the codeHash value, creating a false sense the helper is the source of truth.
findingDetails.recommendation: Either route production codeHash computation through this helper for consistency, or delete it and keep the tests on canonicalFingerprint.

title: Dead export: flattenCompositeTarget is test-only and unused by the statediff planner

findingDetails.evidence: `rg -nw flattenCompositeTarget` returns the definition (statediff.ts:169) and a single test reference (statediff.vitest.ts:56). It is not called by planStatediff (statediff.ts:132-168) nor any production handler that uses createStatediffAction.
findingDetails.why: A public helper exported but never consumed by its own module's planner or production widens the API surface with no purpose and suggests an integration that does not exist.
findingDetails.recommendation: Inline or remove flattenCompositeTarget, or have planStatediff use it if composite-target flattening is meant to be part of the plan path.
